title: Tender Crispy Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A romantic Tunisian dish featuring tender lamb, crispy skin, and miniature marshmallows, with anti-inflammatory properties and umami flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the katsuramuki-cut onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, honey, and miniature marshmallows to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2.5 hours, or until tender and the skin is crispy.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the lamb hot, garnished with fresh herbs and pomegranate seeds if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
